groups.canonical: Bitcoin as a decentralized digital currency without intermediaries
groups.rationale: Unique definition; no other definition states the same claim in equivalent terms.

groups.canonical: Bitcoin: A Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash System
groups.rationale: Unique reference to the Bitcoin whitepaper title; no duplicate reference present.

groups.canonical: Bitcoin creation in 2008 by Satoshi Nakamoto
groups.rationale: Unique historical claim combining creation date and attributed creator.
Unique entity mention; no other entity entry is a strict paraphrase.
Unique comparative throughput claim; no equivalent claim present.
Unique claim about issuance schedule; no strict paraphrase appears elsewhere.
Unique definition of the mining mechanism; no duplicate definition present.
Unique historical claim about Ethereum's proposal and launch dates.
Unique definitional term; no other definition matches it exactly.

groups.canonical: Ethereum energy consumption reduced by approximately 99.95%
groups.rationale: Unique reported result about energy reduction; distinct from the transition claim that caused it.

groups.canonical: Ethereum's transition from Proof of Work to Proof of Stake
groups.rationale: Unique protocol-change claim; distinct from consequences or definitions.

groups.canonical: DeFi TVL peak and decline in 2021
groups.rationale: Unique market-size claim; no equivalent phrasing elsewhere.

groups.canonical: Uniswap pioneered the automated market maker model and launched in 2018
groups.rationale: Unique historical claim about Uniswap's launch and role; distinct from AMM definition and volume metrics.

groups.canonical: Uniswap has processed over $1.8 trillion in trading volume since launch
groups.rationale: Unique quantitative performance claim; no strict paraphrase present.
Unique definition of the AMM model; distinct from claims about Uniswap.

groups.canonical: Programmatic token sales on exchanges not necessarily securities transactions
groups.rationale: Unique legal-result statement; distinct from the cited case reference and broader SEC classification claims.

groups.canonical: SEC classification of most cryptocurrencies as securities
groups.rationale: Unique regulatory claim; not equivalent to the narrower exceptions claim.

groups.canonical: Bitcoin and Ethereum as exceptions to SEC classification
groups.rationale: Unique exceptions claim; related to but not the same as the broader SEC-classification claim.
Unique legal case reference; no duplicate reference appears.
